fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
The ICC Cricket World Cup was first held in 1975 in England, and the West Indies won the inaugural tournament by defeating Australia in the final.
India won the Cricket World Cup in 2011, hosting the tournament on home soil and defeating Sri Lanka in the final at Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai.
Sachin Tendulkar holds the record for the most international runs in cricket history, scoring over 34,000 runs across Test and ODI formats.
The Indian Premier League (IPL), launched in 2008, is the richest and most-watched domestic Twenty20 cricket league in the world.
A standard cricket pitch is 22 yards (20.12 meters) long, and a Test match is played over a maximum of five days.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
Lionel Messi won his first FIFA World Cup title with Argentina in 2022, defeating France on penalties after a 3-3 draw.
The UEFA Champions League is Europe's premier club football competition, with Real Madrid holding the record for most titles.
A standard football match consists of two 45-minute halves, for a total of 90 minutes of regulation play plus stoppage time.
The offside rule states an attacking player is offside if they are nearer to the opponent's goal line than both the ball and the second-last defender when the ball is played to them.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
The Uber Cup is the women's team equivalent of the Thomas Cup, first held in 1956-57, with China being the most successful nation historically.
Badminton was officially introduced as an Olympic sport at the 1992 Barcelona Olympics.
PV Sindhu became the first Indian woman to win an Olympic silver medal in badminton, achieving this at the 2016 Rio Olympics.
A badminton match is typically played as best-of-three games, with each game played to 21 points, requiring a 2-point lead to win.
The shuttlecock, or 'birdie', can travel at speeds exceeding 400 km/h during a smash, making it one of the fastest-moving objects in sport.
The Wimbledon Championships, first held in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world and the only Grand Slam still played on grass.
The four Grand Slam tournaments are the Australian Open, French Open (Roland Garros), Wimbledon, and the US Open.
Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles in tennis history.
Serena Williams won 23 Grand Slam singles titles, the most by any player in the Open Era.
A standard tennis match for men at Grand Slams is best-of-five sets, while women play best-of-three sets.
The French Open is the only Grand Slam played on clay courts, known for producing slower, higher-bouncing rallies.
The NBA (National Basketball Association) was founded in 1946 and is the premier professional basketball league in the world.
The Boston Celtics hold the record for the most NBA championships, with 18 titles as of 2024.
Michael Jordan won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls in the 1990s and is widely regarded as one of the greatest players of all time.
A standard NBA basketball game consists of four quarters of 12 minutes each, for 48 minutes of regulation play.
Basketball was invented in 1891 by Dr. James Naismith, a Canadian physical education instructor, in Springfield, Massachusetts.
The three-point line was introduced to the NBA in the 1979-80 season, changing offensive strategy across the sport.
The Formula 1 World Championship began in 1950, with the first race held at Silverstone, England, and Giuseppe Farina winning the inaugural championship.
Michael Schumacher and Lewis Hamilton share the record for most F1 World Drivers' Championships, each with seven titles.
Ferrari is the oldest and most successful constructor in Formula 1 history, having competed in every season since the championship began in 1950.
Max Verstappen won his first Formula 1 World Championship in 2021 in a dramatic season-deciding finale at the Abu Dhabi Grand Prix.
A standard Formula 1 race distance is calculated to be the smallest number of complete laps exceeding 305 kilometers, except for Monaco.
The points system in modern F1 awards 25 points to the race winner, with points given to the top ten finishers, plus a bonus point for the fastest lap if in the top ten.
